articles.title: Severe heatwave tightens grip till Thursday; widespread rain, hailstorm to lash Chandigarh, Punjab, Haryana from Friday
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Chandigarh
articles.full_text:
Severe heatwave tightens grip till Thursday; widespread rain, hailstorm to lash Chandigarh, Punjab, Haryana from Friday
IMD forecasts 60-70 kmph gusts to batter region from Thursday night; mercury to crash 5-7°C as relief arrives on May 29
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Tuesday issued a special weather bulletin warning of severe heatwave conditions across Chandigarh, Punjab and Haryana through Thursday, even as it forecast widespread rain, thunderstorm, hailstorm, and gusty winds of 60-70 kmph from Friday, bringing the first significant relief in days.
Weather remained dry across Chandigarh, Punjab and Haryana in the past 24 hours. Heatwave conditions persisted at a few places in Haryana, while warm night conditions were recorded at isolated places in the state.
SEVERE HEATWAVE TIGHTENS GRIP
The special bulletin warned of heatwave to severe heatwave conditions across the entire region from Tuesday through Thursday. Temperatures over central, southern, and southwestern Punjab and the whole of Haryana are likely to hover between 45°C and 47°C over the next two days. The rest of Chandigarh and Punjab will sizzle in the range of 42-45°C. No significant change is expected on Wednesday before a sharp fall of 5-7°C sets in over the subsequent three days, the bulletin said. Heatwave conditions are expected to abate from Friday onwards.
BATHINDA, SIRSA HOTTEST IN REGION
Bathinda was the hottest station in Punjab on Tuesday at 45.8°C — slightly above Monday’s 44.7°C. The state’s average maximum temperature edged down marginally by 0.1°C but remained 2.4°C above normal. The lowest minimum temperature in Punjab was 22.1°C, recorded at Amritsar.
In Haryana, Sirsa scorched at 46°C — just a shade below Monday's 46.2°C — with the state average still 2.8°C above normal. The lowest minimum was 23.2°C at Rohtak.
CHANDIGARH FARES BETTER
Chandigarh on Tuesday recorded a maximum temperature of 41.6°C — 1.5°C above normal but 1.1°C lower than Monday’s. The minimum temperature was 22.7°C, a notable 3°C below normal, indicating a slightly less oppressive night. Relative humidity ranged between a parched 10 per cent and 59 per cent.
NEXT FIVE DAYS FOR TRICITY
Wednesday will bring mainly clear skies and the fiercest conditions of the current forecast window, with maximum temperature forecast at 43°C and minimum at 24°C.
Thursday marks the pivot: partly cloudy skies arrive with thunderstorm and light rain possible, while the maximum eases to 40°C and the minimum rises to 26°C. The mercury then falls sharply — to 37°C on Friday, 35°C on Saturday, and 36°C on Sunday — each day with partly cloudy skies.
SEVEN-DAY ALERT OUTLOOK
The orange alert for heatwave to severe heatwave at a few places remains in force on Wednesday. Thursday will carry a simultaneous orange alert for both residual heat wave at isolated places and thunderstorm with lightning with gusty winds of 60-70 kmph and hailstorm at isolated places — a dangerous combination that residents and farmers must prepare for.
From Friday, fairly widespread rain sweeps the region — Chandigarh, Punjab, and Haryana alike — accompanied by thunderstorm and lightning with gusty winds of 60-70 kmph and hailstorm at isolated places. An orange alert is in force. Light to moderate rain is expected at many places on both Friday and Saturday, the special bulletin confirmed.
The orange alert continues on Saturday but the hailstorm threat recedes and gusty winds ease to 40-50 kmph. By Sunday, the alert downgrades to yellow, with only isolated rain, thunderstorm, and 40-50 kmph gusts. From Monday, dry conditions return with no active weather warning issued.
CHANDIGARH REMAINS DRY
There was no rain in the past 24 hours or during afternoon hours. Seasonal rainfall from March 1 stood at 105 mm, which is 101.1 per cent of normal. Sunset on Wednesday is at 7.16 pm and sunrise on Thursday will be at 5.23 am.

articles.title: Intense heat in Haryana, Punjab; Sirsa sizzles at 46 degrees C
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Chandigarh
articles.full_text:
Chandigarh, May 26 (PTI) Haryana and Punjab on Tuesday witnessed intense heat, with Sirsa once again turning out to be the hottest place in the two neighbouring state at a maximum temperature of 46 degrees Celsius.
Haryana’s Sirsa has been reeling under heatwave for the past few days.
Sizzling weather also prevailed in Rohtak and Bhiwani, which recorded respective maximums of 45.6 degrees Celsius and 44.5 degrees Celsius.
Ambala recorded a high of 42.5 degrees Celsius, Hisar 44.5 degrees, Karnal 42.6 degrees while Narnaul recorded 44 degrees Celsius.
Chandigarh, the common capital of the two states, also experienced hot weather at 41.6 degrees Celsius.
In Punjab, Bathinda sizzled at 45.8 degrees Celsius while Patiala registered a maximum of 43.4 degrees Celsius.
Ludhiana lodged a maximum of 42.5 degrees Celsius while Amritsar’s high settled at 41.6 degrees Celsius.
Pathankot, Ferozepur and Mohali recorded respective maximums of 40 degrees, 40.3 degrees and 40 degrees Celsius. PTI SUN NB NB
This report is auto-generated from PTI news service. ThePrint holds no responsibility for its content.

articles.title: IMD issues orange alert for heat wave conditions in Delhi, Haryana, Chandigarh and Punjab - News On AIR
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Chandigarh
articles.full_text:
India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for heat wave conditions today in parts of Delhi, Haryana, Chandigarh, and Punjab. Parts of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Jharkhand, Odisha, and Telangana are also likely to witness severe heat wave conditions during the day. IMD said hot and humid weather will persist today over Gangetic West Bengal. Tonight, East Uttar Pradesh and Vidarbha are likely to experience a warm night.
The Weather Agency also forecast heavy rainfall today in parts of North-east India, Sub Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim, Kerala, Mahe, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry. Lightning and thunderstorms are also expected today in places over Jammu and Kashmir, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Assam, Meghalaya, Bihar, East Uttar Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, and Lakshadweep.

articles.title: पहले फेरे में ही धनबाद-चंडीगढ़ स्पेशल फेल, गर्मी से उबले यात्री; दिल्ली से अंबाला पहुंचने में लगे आठ घंटे - dhanbad chandigarh special fails on maiden run passengers suffer in heat delhi to ambala took eight hours - Jagran
articles.full_text:
पहले फेरे में ही धनबाद-चंडीगढ़ स्पेशल फेल, गर्मी से उबले यात्री; दिल्ली से अंबाला पहुंचने में लगे आठ घंटे
Dhanbad-Chandigarh Special: गर्मी की छुट्टियों में धनबाद-चंडीगढ़ स्पेशल ट्रेन के पहले फेरे में पानी खत्म होने और एसी फेल होने से यात्री परेशान रहे। ...और पढ़ें
HighLights
धनबाद-चंडीगढ़ स्पेशल में पानी खत्म, एसी फेल।
लो बैट्री वोल्टेज के कारण एसी कूलिंग कम।
ट्रेन साढ़े आठ घंटे से अधिक विलंब से पहुंची।
जागरण संवाददाता, धनबाद। गर्मी की छुट्टियों के कारण ट्रेनों में भीड़ बढ़ गई है। भीड़ के कारण ट्रेनों में पानी की डिमांड भी बढ़ गई है। पर रेलवे की अव्यवस्था यात्रियों को परेशान कर रही है। धनबाद से चंडीगढ़ के लिए चलने वाली स्पेशल ट्रेन के पहले फेरे में ही यात्रियों को दुश्वारियां झेलनी पड़ी।
पानी खत्म हो जाने से यात्री परेशान रहे तो एसी फेल हो जाने से कोच हाट चेंबर बना रहा और यात्री उबलते रहे। छोटे बच्चे और बुजुर्ग यात्री ज्यादा परेशान रहे। अधिक किराये के बाद भी सुविधाएं न मिलने से यात्रियों ने सवाल उठाये। यही हाल धनबाद से लोकमान्य तिलक मुंबई जानेवाली ट्रेन का भी रहा। सेकंड एसी में पानी और हैंडवाश उपलब्ध नहीं होने से यात्रियों ने नाराजगी जताई।
रेलवे ने माना लो बैट्री वोल्टेज के कारण कम थी एसी की कूलिंग
धनबाद-चंडीगढ़ स्पेशल में एसी बंद होने की शिकायत पर रेलवे ने माना कि लो बैट्री वोल्टेज के कारण एसी की कूलिंग कम हो गई थी। दूसरी ओर, धनबाद-लोकमान्य तिलक एक्सप्रेस में सिंगरौली स्टेशन पर जलापूर्ति करायी गई। एसी कोच के टायलेट में हैंडवाश भी उपलब्ध कराया गया।
पहले फेरे में साढ़े आठ घंटे लेट, आज शाम में आने की संभावना
धनबाद-चंडीगढ़ स्पेशल अपने फेरे में ही आठ घंटे 22 मिनट लेट रही। तड़के 4:30 के बदले दोपहर लगभग एक बजे चंडीगढ़ पहुंची। वापसी में सोमवार को चंडीगढ़ से सुबह 5:40 के बदले आठ घंटे 35 मिनट विलंब से दोपहर 2:15 पर खुली। मंगलवार सुबह आनेवाली ट्रेन के शाम में आने की संभावना है।
खबरें और भी
200 किमीटर का सफर, आठ घंटे का समय
धनबाद-चंडीगढ़ स्पेशल ट्रेन ने दिल्ली से अंबाला पहुंचने में करीब आठ घंटे का समय ली। यह ट्रेन रात एक बजर नाै मिनट पर दिल्ली रेलवे स्टेशन पहुंच गई थी।

सुबह नाै बजकर 13 मिनट पर पानीपत और दोपहर 12 बजे अंबाला पहुंची। दिल्ली और अंबाला के बीच की दूरी करीब 200 किलोमीटर है।
